title: SOC 2 CC9 — Change Authorization Dual-Control on Privileged Cloud Roles

compliance.assurance_rationale: SOC 2 CC8.1 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOC 2 CC8.1 (Change management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.8.26: SOC 2 CC9 — Change Authorization Dual-Control on Privileged Cloud Roles.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.8.26' running on sourcetype aws:cloudtrail and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
spl:
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" eventName IN ("AttachRolePolicy","PutRolePolicy") earliest=-7d
| eval ticket=mvindex(split(requestParameters.policyArn,"/"),-1)
| join type=left userIdentity.arn [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" u_dual_approval=true earliest=-14d
    | rename opened_by as approver1, u_second_approver as approver2
    | eval join_key=opened_by
    | table number, join_key
  ]
| where isnull(number)
| stats count by userIdentity.arn, eventName
| sort - count
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Change data model (Change.All_Changes).
description: CC9 addresses mitigation of fraud and management override. Ensuring IAM role attachments and policy updates have dual approval in PAM or ticketing prevents single-person override of segregation of duties—key for change authorization narratives.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for SOC 2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes aws, itsm and sourcetypes aws:cloudtrail, snow:change_request. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for CC9 — Change Authorization Dual-Control on Privileged Cloud Roles. SOC 2 is how we show we run our services responsibly, and we keep evidence that supports that story.
implementation: (1) Replace join with robust ticket id extracted from CloudTrail `requestParameters` or session context; (2) integrate CyberArk session id where available; (3) emergency break-glass via lookup with post-incident review flag; (4) SOX-style quarterly sampling export; (5) align with CC6 logical access monitoring. Indexes required: `index=aws`, `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail`, `sourcetype=snow:change_request`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"` eventName IN ("AttachRolePolicy","PutRolePolicy") (requestParameters, userIdentity.arn, _time); `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` with `u_dual_approval=true`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Replace join with robust ticket id extracted from CloudTrail `requestParameters` or session context; (2) integrate CyberArk session id where available; (3) emergency break-glass via lookup with post-incident review flag; (4) SOX-style quarterly sampling export; (5) align with CC6 logical access monitoring.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" eventName IN ("AttachRolePolicy","PutRolePolicy") earliest=-7d
| eval ticket=mvindex(split(requestParameters.policyArn,"/"),-1)
| join type=left userIdentity.arn [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" u_dual_approval=true earliest=-14d
    | rename opened_by as approver1, u_second_approver as approver2
    | eval join_key=opened_by
    | table number, join_key
  ]
| where isnull(number)
| stats count by userIdentity.arn, eventName
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOC 2 CC9 — Change Authorization Dual-Control on Privileged Cloud Roles** — CC9 addresses mitigation of fraud and management override. Ensuring IAM role attachments and policy updates have dual approval in PAM or ticketing prevents single-person override of segregation of duties—key for change authorization narratives.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"` eventName IN ("AttachRolePolicy","PutRolePolicy") (requestParameters, userIdentity.arn, _time); `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` with `u_dual_approval=true`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: aws; **sourcetype**: aws:cloudtrail. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=aws, sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ticket** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(number)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **userIdentity.arn, eventName**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**SOC 2 CC9 — Change Authorization Dual-Control on Privileged Cloud Roles** — CC9 addresses mitigation of fraud and management override. Ensuring IAM role attachments and policy updates have dual approval in PAM or ticketing prevents single-person override of segregation of duties—key for change authorization narratives.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"` eventName IN ("AttachRolePolicy","PutRolePolicy") (requestParameters, userIdentity.arn, _time); `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` with `u_dual_approval=true`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: approver1, eventName, join_key, number, ticket, userIdentity.arn.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unauthorized IAM changes), Single value (count), Timeline.
visualization: Table (unauthorized IAM changes), Single value (count), Timeline. Key fields: `approver1`, `eventName`, `join_key`, `number`, `ticket`, `userIdentity.arn`.
knownFalsePositives: Evidence freshness lags during quarterly cycles, control owner transitions, or platform changes.
